gaps: No test covers the Voyager Legend 50 device at all — all existing language-triggered DFU tests (66363556, 66363557, 66363558, 66363559, 66363583, 66363596) are specific to Voyager Free 60+ (TWS earbuds with smart charge case), not the Voyager Legend 50 (a Bluetooth headset with a standard charge case).

No test verifies whether a language-triggered DFU on Voyager Legend 50 updates the charge case firmware at all — the bug scenario specifically checks if the charge case firmware version matches the headset firmware version after DFU, which is a unique assertion not covered by any existing test.

No test covers the scenario where a language change triggers a DFU on a single-component device (headset only) and then checks the charge case separately — existing tests focus on multi-component devices (earbuds + case + stand) where the case is a smart device with its own firmware.

No test validates the post-DFU state comparison between headset firmware version and charge case firmware version on Voyager Legend 50 — this is a distinct verification point not present in any existing test case.

generated_testcases.title: TC-VL50-LANG-DFU-001: Verify charge case firmware version matches headset firmware version after language-triggered DFU on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 is fully charged and connected to the phone via Bluetooth. Poly Studio Mobile app is installed and logged in.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Legend 50 device name from the device list.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information.
Record the current Headset Firmware Version (e.g., 1.2.3).
Record the current Charge Case Firmware Version (e.g., 1.2.3).
Navigate back to the main device screen.
Tap Audio Settings → Language Settings.
Change the language setting from the current value to a different language (e.g., from English to French).
Observe the prompt: 'Changing language will trigger a firmware update. Continue?' — Tap 'Continue' or 'Yes'.
Wait for the DFU process to complete. Do not interrupt the update. The device may disconnect and reconnect during the process.
Once the update completes and the device reconnects, navigate to Settings → Device Information.
Record the new Headset Firmware Version.
Record the new Charge Case Firmware Version.
Compare the Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version.
generated_testcases.expected: The Charge Case Firmware Version matches the Headset Firmware Version exactly. Both versions are updated to the same build number (e.g., both show 1.4.0). No mismatch or discrepancy between the two firmware versions.

generated_testcases.title: TC-VL50-LANG-DFU-002: Verify charge case firmware version is reported correctly after language-triggered DFU on Voyager Legend 50 (edge case: downgrade scenario)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 is connected. Poly Studio Mobile app is open on the device screen.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information.
Record the current Headset Firmware Version (e.g., 2.0.0).
Record the current Charge Case Firmware Version (e.g., 2.0.0).
Navigate back to the main device screen.
Tap Audio Settings → Language Settings.
Change the language setting to a language that triggers a DFU to an older firmware version (e.g., from English to a language whose firmware bundle is version 1.8.0).
Tap 'Continue' on the DFU prompt.
Wait for the DFU process to complete fully.
After reconnection, navigate to Settings → Device Information.
Record the new Headset Firmware Version.
Record the new Charge Case Firmware Version.
Verify both versions are identical.
generated_testcases.expected: Both Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version show the same version number (e.g., 1.8.0). The charge case firmware is downgraded in sync with the headset firmware. No version mismatch.

generated_testcases.title: TC-VL50-LANG-DFU-003: Verify charge case firmware remains functional after language-triggered DFU on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 is connected. Poly Studio Mobile app is open.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information and record the pre-update Headset and Charge Case firmware versions.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Language Settings.
Change the language to trigger a DFU (e.g., from English to German).
Confirm the DFU prompt and wait for completion.
After the device reconnects, navigate to Settings → Device Information and confirm both firmware versions match.
Place the headset into the charge case.
Close the charge case lid.
Open the charge case lid and remove the headset.
Verify the headset powers on and reconnects to the phone automatically.
Place a test call and verify audio is heard in the earpiece.
Verify the charge case LED indicates charging status correctly (e.g., solid red when charging, green when fully charged).
Connect the charge case to a USB-C power source and verify the charge case LED responds appropriately.
generated_testcases.expected: The charge case charges the headset normally (LED indicators function correctly). The headset powers on and reconnects automatically when removed from the case. Call audio works correctly. The charge case firmware update did not break any charge case hardware functionality.

generated_testcases.title: TC-VL50-LANG-DFU-004: Verify charge case firmware version persists after app restart and device reconnect post-language-triggered DFU
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 is connected. Poly Studio Mobile app is open.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information and record the pre-update Headset and Charge Case firmware versions.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Language Settings.
Change the language to trigger a DFU (e.g., from English to Spanish).
Confirm the DFU prompt and wait for completion.
After reconnection, navigate to Settings → Device Information and record the post-update Headset and Charge Case firmware versions. Confirm they match.
Force-close the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe it away from the recent apps list).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Legend 50 device name.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information.
Record the Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version.
Compare with the versions recorded immediately after the DFU.
generated_testcases.expected: The Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version displayed after app restart are identical to the versions recorded immediately after the DFU. The firmware versions persist correctly across app restarts.

generated_testcases.title: TC-VL50-LANG-DFU-005: Verify charge case firmware version persists after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect post-language-triggered DFU
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 is connected. Poly Studio Mobile app is open.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information and record the pre-update Headset and Charge Case firmware versions.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Language Settings.
Change the language to trigger a DFU (e.g., from English to Italian).
Confirm the DFU prompt and wait for completion.
After reconnection, navigate to Settings → Device Information and record the post-update Headset and Charge Case firmware versions. Confirm they match.
On the phone, go to Bluetooth settings and tap 'Forget' or 'Unpair' the Voyager Legend 50.
Wait 5 seconds.
Put the Voyager Legend 50 into pairing mode (press and hold the Bluetooth button for 4 seconds until the LED flashes red/blue).
Re-pair the headset with the phone via Bluetooth settings.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Legend 50 device name.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information.
Record the Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version.
Compare with the versions recorded immediately after the DFU.
generated_testcases.expected: The Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version displayed after Bluetooth disconnect and re-pair are identical to the versions recorded immediately after the DFU. The firmware versions persist correctly across Bluetooth state transitions.

generated_testcases.title: TC-VL50-LANG-DFU-006: Verify charge case firmware version is correctly reported when language-triggered DFU is performed on Voyager Legend 50 with minimum battery level
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 headset and charge case are at approximately 20% battery level (low but sufficient for DFU). Phone battery is above 50%.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Legend 50 device name.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information and record the pre-update Headset and Charge Case firmware versions.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Language Settings.
Change the language to trigger a DFU (e.g., from English to Portuguese).
Confirm the DFU prompt and wait for completion.
After reconnection, navigate to Settings → Device Information.
Record the new Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version.
Verify both versions match.
generated_testcases.expected: The DFU completes successfully even at low battery (20%). The Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version match after the update. No corruption or incomplete update occurs due to low battery conditions.

generated_testcases.title: TC-VL50-LANG-DFU-007: Verify charge case firmware version is NOT updated when language-triggered DFU is cancelled mid-process on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 is connected. Poly Studio Mobile app is open.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information and record the pre-update Headset Firmware Version (e.g., 1.2.3) and Charge Case Firmware Version (e.g., 1.2.3).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Language Settings.
Change the language to trigger a DFU (e.g., from English to Dutch).
When the DFU prompt appears, tap 'Cancel' or 'No'.
Verify the app returns to the Language Settings screen without initiating any update.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information.
Record the Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version.
Compare with the pre-cancellation versions.
generated_testcases.expected: Both the Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version remain unchanged from their pre-cancellation values (e.g., both still show 1.2.3). No firmware update was applied to either component because the DFU was cancelled.

generated_testcases.title: TC-VL50-LANG-DFU-008: Verify charge case firmware version after language-triggered DFU followed by a second language-triggered DFU on Voyager Legend 50
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 is connected. Poly Studio Mobile app is open.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information and record the initial Headset and Charge Case firmware versions.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Language Settings.
Change the language to trigger DFU #1 (e.g., from English to French).
Confirm and wait for DFU #1 to complete.
After reconnection, navigate to Settings → Device Information and record the post-DFU#1 Headset and Charge Case firmware versions. Confirm they match.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Language Settings.
Change the language again to trigger DFU #2 (e.g., from French to German).
Confirm the DFU prompt and wait for DFU #2 to complete.
After reconnection, navigate to Settings → Device Information.
Record the post-DFU#2 Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version.
Verify both versions match each other.
Verify the versions are different from the pre-DFU#1 versions (i.e., the update actually occurred).
generated_testcases.expected: After two consecutive language-triggered DFUs, the Headset Firmware Version and Charge Case Firmware Version still match each other exactly. Both components are updated in sync through multiple DFU cycles. No version drift occurs between headset and charge case.
